tweet: I intend to fulfill that promise to Sarah’s loving parents – Michelle Root and Scott Root – the promise that I will do everything I can to ensure that not one more parent has to go through what the Roots have faced…the loss of both their daughter and the promise of justice.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: This bill isn’t about the southern border or the wall or visa numbers or the larger immigration debate at all…this bill is about changing the system for the better and ensuring that families have the promise of justice.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Despite provisions of Sarah’s Law being put in place by executive order, it is critical that the Senate codify these enforcement priorities, so that they cannot be removed by future administrations.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Here’s a bit of good news: President Trump implemented parts of Sarah’s Law through an executive order in 2017, including directing the Secretary of Homeland Security to prioritize the removal of violent criminals.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Sarah’s Law is about as commonsense a reform as there is. It recognizes the simple fact that all criminals should be held accountable for their actions, and not simply allowed to slip back into the shadows.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Sarah’s Law is simple: it requires that ICE take custody of a person who is in the country illegally if they are charged w/ a crime that seriously injures another person.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Today, I rise to call upon my Senate colleagues to end this senseless madness. To do something and stop another tragedy like this from happening with a simple and clean fix. I am asking the Senate to join 26 of their colleagues and pass my bill, Sarah’s Law.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Sadly, what happened to Sarah is not an isolated incident. We have seen this story play out time and time again in the more than four years since Sarah’s killing. Innocent lives taken by criminals that entered the U.S. illegally through a porous border—but nothing ever changes.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Mejia posted bond, was released, and disappeared. Still, more than 4 years later, he remains a fugitive – denying Sarah’s loved ones any sense of justice or closure. As a mother, I cannot fathom the grief that her family and friends continue to feel after such a devastating loss.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Despite repeated requests by local law enforcement, Immigration and Customs Enforcement failed to detain Mejia because of a nonsensical policy that allows ICE to use discretion when determining whether to detain a criminal alien charged with a violent offense.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: In Iowa, we’ve learned this in a very tragic way.   On January 31st, 2016 – the same day as her college graduation – Sarah Root was killed by an illegal immigrant named Edwin Mejia. He was drag racing with a blood alcohol level more than three times the legal limit.

name: Joni Ernst

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: To prevent buyers’ remorse, I’m giving everyone in Washington fair warning: My office will be reviewing your last minute purchases and asking you to justify them to the taxpayers. It’s time to put an end to this reckless behavior.  Billion-dollar binge buying is no way to budget.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: With our national debt now surpassing $22 trillion, Washington should be looking for ways to save by canceling or delaying unnecessary expenses, rather than splurging on end-of-year wish lists.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: This bill won’t end all wasteful spending, but it will force agencies to put more thought into long term planning and curtail the bad habit of out-of-control impulsive spending.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: That’s why earlier this year I introduced the End-of-Year Fiscal Responsibility Act, which would limit an agency’s spending in the last two months of the year to no more than the average it spent per month during the first ten months of the year.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Last year’s $804 billion budget deficit could have been wiped out and turned into a surplus if the unobligated balances being held in federal coffers had been canceled.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: This year, it’s estimated the government will end up with more than $825 billion in unspent funds that have not been committed by contract or otherwise obligated to be spent.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: And what are we as Congress doing about this wasteful spending? Nada. Congress is sitting idly by letting Washington bureaucrats waste the hard-earned dollars of folks in my home state of #Iowa.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: -$4.6 million on lobster tail and crab; -$2.1 million on games, toys, and wheeled goods; -Over $53,000 on china tableware; -More than $40,000 on clocks; -And nearly $12,000 for a commercial foosball table!

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: And let me tell you folks – #Iowans, and hardworking folks across the country, should be appalled by many of the last minute purchases our tax dollars are paying for.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: If not spent by midnight on September 30th, leftover dollars expire and can no longer be used. Rather than returning the money to taxpayers, binge buying bureaucrats are wasting billions of taxpayer dollars needlessly.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair The VA conducted a review of all their providers and found a small number of providers who were hired with revoked licenses. My bill focuses on that small group of providers. It’s a targeted oversight measure, with minimal costs, that will give our veterans peace of mind.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair The Ensuring Quality Care for Our Veterans Act ensures that every provider who was hired with a revoked license will have their care scrutinized by a neutral, third party.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair Every veteran who was treated by a physician with a revoked license should have their medical care scrutinized by a neutral third-party. That’s exactly what my bill does.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair Dr. Schneider never should have been hired to treat our veterans. While the VA reformed its hiring practices, there are still veterans out there who were treated by physicians with revoked licenses but who don’t know if they received bad care.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair All of this changed on December 3, 2017. A disturbing report in USA Today found that the VA had knowingly hired providers with revoked medical licenses and who have a history of providing substandard care. One of those providers was Dr. Schneider.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair However, in the weeks and months following the surgery, it became clear to Anthony that something was wrong. His health hadn’t improved, and Dr. Schneider just said that it was part of the “symptoms” from the surgery.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair Anthony was referred to a neurosurgeon at the Iowa City VA, Dr. Schneider. Dr. Schneider performed surgery and stated he had removed all of the brain tumor.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: @VAChair Today, I want to share the story of Anthony, an #Iowa veteran. In 2017, Anthony was experiencing headaches so he went to the Iowa City VA Medical Center to get an MRI of his head and neck. Anthony’s MRI results came back. Tragically, they indicated he had a brain tumor.

name: Joni Ernst
tweet: Soon, I'll be speaking at the @VAChair hearing on my bill, the Ensuring Quality Care for Our Veterans Act, which ensures a third party reviews each case where a veteran was treated by a VA provider who was later found to have a revoked license. Tune in:  https://t.co/Qy7wnZMYwt
